text.title: REST API Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of REST API Design. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Routes like `/api/getUser`, `/api/doRefund`; codegen produces unnatural client methods.
antipattern.root-cause: Verbs in URL, methods inverted from semantics.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite to noun paths; map verbs to HTTP methods; ship via OpenAPI for clean codegen.

antipattern.symptom: 200 OK with `{error: 'not found'}` body; client SDK does not retry, monitoring dashboards green during outage.
antipattern.fix: Switch to 404/400/422/etc.; clients and observability behave correctly.

antipattern.symptom: Page 500 of /v1/events takes 8 seconds; DB CPU at 90%.
antipattern.fix: Switch to cursor pagination keyed on created_at + id.

antipattern.symptom: Breaking change shipped to /api/users; mobile clients crash.
antipattern.root-cause: No version path; clients had no way to pin v1.
antipattern.fix: Introduce /v1; deprecate old path with sunset header; document migration window.
